audit_items.proof_trace: The property requires all pointer dereferences in rule.c to be preceded by null checks. At L157, `for ( p = flagstring; p[ 0 ] != '\0'; p++ )` dereferences `flagstring` without any null guard, despite the explicit comment at L140-143 stating 'A null flagstring should be allowed' (all other parameters have `assert(X != NULL)` guards at L136-139). The only current caller (config.c:254) always passes `""` rather than NULL, so the bug is latent — no current external path exercises the crash.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If any caller passes NULL as `flagstring` to `rewrite_rule_compile` (as the documented API contract permits), the immediate dereference at L157 causes a NULL pointer dereference crash; a future internal caller or an API extension trusting the documented NULL-safe contract would trigger this.
